_meta.purpose: Virology rapid diagnostic test (RDT / lateral-flow cassette) reading -> result + interpretation. The `virology_rdt` detector locates the control (C) and test (T) lines (or result classes); the vision service derives POSITIVE / NEGATIVE / INVALID. Decision support only; a scientist confirms and the assay's confirmatory algorithm applies (e.g. HIV confirmatory testing, HBsAg neutralisation).
_meta.logic: C + T lines = REACTIVE (positive); C only = non-reactive (negative); no C line = INVALID (repeat with a new device). Read within the kit's time window (typically 15 min; do not read after 30 min).
_meta.source: WHO/CDC lateral-flow RDT reading guidance. Curated for JORINOVA NEXUS.

control_line.name: Control (C) line present
control_line.note: No control line = INVALID regardless of the T line.

test_line.name: Test (T) line present
test_line.disease: Reactive band for the target analyte
test_line.note: Reactive only if a control line is also present.

positive.name: Reactive (positive) result
positive.note: CRITICAL: apply the confirmatory algorithm before reporting/diagnosing.

negative.name: Non-reactive (negative) result
negative.note: A negative RDT does not exclude early/window-period infection.

invalid.name: Invalid result
invalid.note: No/weak control line, or a test line without a control line. REPEAT with a new device + fresh specimen.

hiv_reactive.name: HIV RDT reactive
hiv_reactive.note: CRITICAL: confirm per national HIV testing algorithm (2nd/3rd assay); never diagnose on one RDT.

hbsag_reactive.name: HBsAg RDT reactive
hbsag_reactive.note: Confirm with HBsAg neutralisation / HBV serology + viral load.

covid_positive.name: SARS-CoV-2 antigen RDT positive
covid_positive.note: Antigen RDT; correlate clinically, PCR if discordant.

dengue_ns1_positive.name: Dengue NS1 RDT positive
dengue_ns1_positive.note: NS1 positive = acute infection; pair with IgM/IgG.

syphilis_reactive.name: Syphilis (Treponemal) RDT reactive
syphilis_reactive.note: Treponemal test stays reactive for life; confirm activity with RPR/VDRL titre.
